entry.title: Re: SAP learning exercise- no access
entry.content: Hi, seems that the issue has now been solved. It is now working again.

entry.title: Re: Easily add Postgres to Data Warehouse Cloud (and non SAP Cloud Apps) using Open Connectors
entry.content:
Hello @JonGooding ,

I found this note that basically say "not all open connectors are working with Datasphere".

"API list on the open connectors is not supported"

https://me.sap.com/notes/3445022/E

do we have a list of what connectors are working?

 

Best regards,

Alberto

entry.title: Re: Tutorials for Creating a full stack CAP application u...
entry.content:
Did you have a look at https://github.com/SAP-samples/cloud-cap-samples/? SAP uses as an example of how you can structure different component in different projects (even though in this example it is in on repo).  

You can have a look at he CAP website or DJ Adams posts and videos to learn more about plugins. 

I personally use the plugin mechanism to string together 9 modules. 

If you don't want to deploy them together but see each one as a standalone micro service that build on it, you can use the MTA and some destination magic to make that happen.

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
Thanks Joe,

I am also interested in knowing when the 2025 federal tax tables will be released by BSI.

Debbie

entry.title: Re: Why does 0RECORDMODE exist ? What is its usecase ?
entry.content:
It's needed for deltas, so the before, after and reverse/delete images can be identified and processed accordingly.

For example in a DSO with Changelog without the corresponding before/after pairs, it would not be possible to rollback requests, since then the system would not know what the previous value was.

entry.content:
Hi @ABC1231231 , it looks like you got some help. If your question was answered, please accept the solution. Let us know if you need any more information.
Thanks!

entry.title: Re: BW reporting preview freezes in Eclipse
entry.content: While you do the reporting preview in Eclipse, is there any activity in the backend on the ABAP application server?

entry.title: Looking for Job opportunities in SAP Successfactors Employee Central
entry.content:
Dear Friends,

I have 4 yrs exp in HR and as SAP SF End User(Fieldglass). I am trying to shift my career into SAP. I am trained and certified in SAP SF Employee Central and Time Management modules. Looking for some real-time experience. Pls let me know if you have any job opportunities or internships available in Hyderabad, India or Remote. I can join immediately.  

#SAPSuccessfactors #Employeecentral #jobs

Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: Re: How to find data source, extractor, CDS views for tim...
entry.content:
If you want to see Transformation mappings, you can use RSTRANFIELD.

If you want to see the field definition of Datasources, you can use RSDSSEGFD.

For ABAP dictionary you could try DD03L but that will not have CDS.

I think if you join DDLDEPENDENCY with DD03L you can get the CDS views, which are using ABAP tables/views as sources and which have the desired fields in them.

entry.title: Re: Upgrade Mobile Application Integration Framework (MAIF) using SAP Note Transport-based Correctio
entry.content:
Hi,

In the TCI notes is mentioned "If you import this TCI in your system and at later point you import an FPS/SPS package that exceeds the validity range of an implemented TCI, all corrections of the TCI are imported with the FPS/SPS package, and the TCI becomes obsolete."

 

Does this have impact on S4H upgrades where config/coding will be lost or require additional actions on SPAU during the upgrade?

Best regards,

Dirk

entry.title: Re: SAP IQ 16.1 - Which tools can you recommend to track...

entry.title: Error when reading a released CDS view from a released BADI
entry.content:
Hi,

I am getting this error whenever I retrieve data via a released CDS view with access control from a released BADI for VA02.

S4 version: Private Cloud 2023 FPS00

Enhancement spot: ES_SD_SLS_EXTEND

BADI: SD_SLS_CHECK_BEFORE_SAVE

We are selecting from I_BusinessPartner CDS view. Is there anyway to achieve this in Tier 1 or we have to create our own custom CDS view without access control setup?

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite OData V4 receiving format JSON
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to create Sales Order in SAP Public Cloud with help of Sales Order Integration, SAP_COM_0109. I am getting error "Unexpected character '{' (code 123) in prolog at [row,col {unknown-source}]: [1,2]". In note 3266527 stays: "Use XML". Why? When I run Postmen with same endpoint and JSON body I am able to create Sales Order. Why in iFlow I must convert it to XML or there is some way to enable JSON as accepted format?

 

Thank you

entry.title: User login take long time
entry.content:
Dears,

Professional users  take long time during login ( About 7 Minutes) 

But limited users don't take long time ( about 30 seconds only )

SAP Business One, Version for HANA

Please, advise

Many thanks

entry.title: SAP Datasphere and Open connectors what is supposed to be "access method BULK" ?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a very slow Data Flow in Datasphere, due to the fact that a source is from an Open Connector connection.

The Dataflow is very slow, i don't know if it is due to the connection between the source and SAP Integration suite, or between SAP integration suite and dataflows, or inside the dataflow itself.

What is my idea based on observations?

With open connectors the datatypes set for column of type STRING is "STRING(5000)"  (for every STRING columns in the source!!!).

So a possible problem that I found is that when I raise batch size the dataflow => it runs into an error and say "out of memory". This makes me think that dataflow for some reason preallocate memory based on datatypes of the columns. Many columns like "IDs" that may have up to 10 character are treated as STRING(5000).

And this datatype can not be changed in the source property inside Datasphere.

But this is all hypothetical based on observations.

Another observation is that the first rows are written with high speed, the last rows are incredibly slow. this makes me think that the extraction made from the open connector do something like this:

 

read the first 1000 rows and process them,

read the first 2000 rows and process the last 1000.

..

read the first 100000 rows and process the last 1000.

I believe the rows are fetched multiple times from a source and only the last are processed, and this due to the times it takes to download the content (around 200K rows x 100 columns (many of which have a STRING datatype)).

 

A thing that I notice is that we have options inside dataflows that are specific for Open Connectors.

and that are not documented in me.sap.com nor in help.sap.com.

 

So my question is what exactly do the option "Access Method" under the Advanced panel of a source of a dataflow in case of Open connectors?

 

Best Regards,

Alberto

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA 2022 What’s New – Target PSA in EWM with Automatic WT creation.
entry.content:
Hey, 

Thanks for the article. Do you know if it is possible to have target PSA functionality with RF and with Fixed HU

entry.title: Re: Cash Management: Liquidity Item Derivation in MIRO Tr...
entry.content:
I will explain my requirement in more detail.

Business Case (simple example)

Material A needs to be treated as Liquidity Item A (Operating Exp 1) and Material B needs to be treated as Liquidity Item B (Operating Exp 2). This can be achieved using Queries (Origin X).

For MIRO, the Accounting Entry would be GR/IR Acc Dr, Vendor Acc Cr. For Vendor Line item we can get the Liquidity Item using Queries (Origin C), but how to get the Liquidity Item for GR/IR Account.  Using a default in FLQINFACC will not give me correct representation (as shown above, liquidity item needs to change based on Material). Request your help for the same.

What is SAP's solution for deriving Liquidity Item in MIRO transaction (which needs to derive based on the contents of the Purchase Order)

 

@Vibha_Tiwary 

@Hongjun_Qian

entry.title: Re: Can someone explain me the Parameter EFB
entry.content: Thank you Dominik for sharing your knowledge that helped a lot!

entry.title: Re: S_ALR_87011964 display ANLA-GDGRP and ANLB-NDJAR

entry.title: Re: EWM - Method or function module to delete stock refer...
entry.content:
I would not advise that to do as it is against standard behavior  because it is risky. I would say at once you need to confirm with SAP support team. Raise your scenario to SAP support and take advise 

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Re: Error when reading a released CDS view from a released BADI
entry.content: I encountered the same error when had the same code inside SAVE_MODIFIED of a custom RAP BO behavior definition with strict (2) setting.

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for Java 8.10 on the Horizon
entry.content:
Hi @raghug Thank you for taking time and reply to my issue. I did what you suggested but still same error is popping up.

GUI is not taking this configuration from File, it's from somewhere else. I downloaded new copy of SAP GUI JAVA 8.10 and run, without even Importing any connections, it still shows that File and Fatal error displays.

One thing wanted to mention - I have Imported this connection file, these are not manually added connections.

Thanks-

entry.title: UDM_DISPUTE: Does anyone know the usage of "List location"
entry.content: In UDM_DISPUTE , in S4 systems, we see this icon "list location". Is anyone aware of the usage of this feature?

entry.title: Unlocking Business Efficiency: A Deep Dive into Filters in SAP Signavio Process Insights, Discovery
entry.content:
Please click on the images to see them in good quality

As businesses strive for greater efficiency in an increasingly competitive landscape, the ability to identify bottlenecks, inefficiencies, and opportunities for process improvement is more critical than ever. SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition, offers a robust yet lightweight tool for analyzing process performance in SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA systems. Among its many features, the filters for analyzing process data enables targeted insights that drive actionable decisions.

In this blog, we’ll explore the key filters—that I name the “What, Where, When”—that make SAP Signavio Process Insights indispensable for root cause analysis and process performance improvement. These filters empower users to identify the “what,” “where,” and “when” of inefficiencies, providing a structured and visual approach to uncovering actionable insights.

 

Why Filters Matter in Process Performance Analysis

Before diving into the filters, it’s important to understand their overarching value. Businesses today generate vast amounts of data, making it challenging to focus on the specific elements that impact process efficiency. SAP Signavio Process Insights addresses this challenge with powerful filters that allow users to:

  • Pinpoint inefficiencies by focusing on specific document types, locations, or timeframes.
  • Simplify root cause analysis with detailed breakdowns of performance indicators and blockers.
  • Identify and prioritize areas for improvement by isolating outdated or problematic process data.

By using these filters, organizations can accelerate decision-making, reduce costs, and enhance overall operational performance.


Exploring the Filters: What, Where, When

Let’s explore each filter in detail, including how they work, what they uncover, and how they can be used to improve process performance.

Let’s see a concrete example together:

When you enter any Process Performance indicator (PPI), any blocker, process step or Process Flow, you will be able to filter the information by clicking on the “filter” button on the top right corner.

You can see here an example for the PPI “Overdue and Open Account Receivable items”:

“Overdue and Open Account Receivable items” will be contracted to “AR items” in the rest of the blog – Quick refresh: the AR items are the invoices that should already have been paid by your customers, and were not-.


By clicking on it, a new page will display with the available filters (side note: every filters available in the full edition of SAP Signavio Process Insights will be displayed, some of these are not available in SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition).

By default, the filters for Company Code and Document Type, will be shown:


So our “What” and our “Where”, which makes our life easy.

For each filter selection, a new table will be displayed with the segmentation for the related filter, meaning how many items belong to the specific topic (business area, type, date, etc….).

You can directly see in this example that a big part of the AR items happen for the document types “DR”, “RV” and “DZ” and for the Company Codes “F001” and “F002”.

To go a bit more in details, let’s remove the filter “Company Code” by clicking on the bin symbol above the the related table.


1. What: Understanding Document-Level Insights

The What filter focuses on identifying the type of document involved in a process.

By applying the What filter, users can quickly assess:

  • Which document types are associated with inefficiencies. For instance, if a high number of overdue invoices are flagged, it could indicate bottlenecks in the payment approval process.
  • Patterns across documents, such as recurring delays in specific categories, allowing users to focus their improvement efforts.

 

Benefits of the “What” Filter:

  • Precision: Isolate inefficiencies tied to specific document types without sifting through irrelevant data.
  • Root Cause Identification: Understand which step in the document’s lifecycle is contributing to delays or errors.

By leveraging the “What” filter, businesses can uncover inefficiencies at a granular level, enabling targeted interventions to streamline processes.


Demo:
Here is an example:

 

Out of the 112,292 AR items, let’s focus on the document types that are the most represented for this PPI, the onces for which there are more than 10,000 items:

 

By clicking on the Ok button on the right corner, you will see the list of the AR items related to the selected document types:

You can then export this list for further investigation with the related process experts.


2. Where: Locating Inefficiencies by Geography and Organization

The Where filter pinpoints inefficiencies based on location, company code, or plant. This is particularly valuable for organizations operating across multiple geographies or business units, where process performance may vary significantly.

 

Examples of insights derived from the Where filter include:

  • Identifying company codes with unusually high processing times, indicating potential training or resource allocation issues.
  • Highlighting plants where delays in production orders impact downstream supply chain processes.
  • Detecting inefficiencies in regional operations that may stem from differing local practices or compliance challenges.

 

Benefits of the “Where” Filter:

  • Visibility: Gain a clear view of geographic or organizational hotspots where processes lag behind.
  • Targeted Action: Focus resources on specific locations or teams that need improvement.
  • Scalability: Evaluate global operations holistically while addressing local inefficiencies.

With the Where filter, businesses can optimize performance across their organizational landscape, ensuring consistency and efficiency across locations.

 

Demo:
Here, we removed our filter for “Document Type” and added “Company Code” from the filter selection table on the left:

 

As before, out of the 112,292 AR items, let’s focus on the company codes that are the most represented for this PPI, the onces for which there are more than 10,000 items:

 

By clicking on the Ok button on the right corner, you will see the list of the AR items related to the selected company codes:

You can then export this list for further investigation with the related business area.


3. When: Identifying Temporal Patterns and Trends

The When filter allows users to analyze process performance based on time, providing insights into both current and historical data. Key capabilities include:

  • Highlighting new issues that may signal ongoing problems in process performance, such as an increase in overdue orders over the past quarter.
  • Identifying obsolete data that can be archived, freeing up system resources and improving data clarity.

 

Benefits of the “When” Filter:

  • Proactive Management: Address emerging issues before they escalate into larger problems.
  • Data Hygiene: Ensure outdated or irrelevant information is removed from active systems, reducing clutter and improving data accuracy.

The When filter equips businesses to make time-sensitive decisions, ensuring that their processes remain agile and efficient in a dynamic business environment.

Demo:
Here, we removed our filter for  “Company Code”  and we can add a new filter from the  filter selection table on the left.

Let’s first use “Fiscal Year”. This will help us to quickly segment the data per year and clearly and quickly assess if the AR items are currently happening or are obsolete data:

We can see that the majority of the items are old and not so much business relevant.

You can of course filter and export your selction like for the two previous examples, but I would like to do something else.

 

Let’s select “Due on” as a new filter:

 

You see that in the the related table list, there is an entey for each date. This is very convenient if you are looking for a specific date, like if you wish to review the AR items from the 17th December 2021.

By clicking on “ID” a drop-down menu will appear:

In this drop-down menu, select “Advanced Filtering”.

A new pop-up will be show:

 

You can select the data from a specific date (“From”), until a specific date (“To”),  or even a specific time range (“From/To”) and for the last X days.

Let’s select  from the case from 31st December 2021:

 

The various dates will be shown:

 

We see that there are in total 1,004 AR items between 31st December 2021 and today, which were due 5 different dates.

 

Bringing It All Together

The true power of SAP Signavio Process Insights lies in its ability to combine these filters into a comprehensive analysis. For example, a user could analyze:

  • When: 1. Archive obsolete data (keep in this filter only what is less than six month old)
  • What: Most impacted document types (> 10k)
  • Where: In a specific company code or plant (> 10k)

Where before we use the “OR” logic (filter by “Document type” or “Company code” or “Due on”), we use here the “AND” logic (what are the AR items related to specific document types, coming from specific company codes in the last 6 months).

And we see the number of AR items fulfilling these criteria are only 962 out of 112,292:

 

This targeted approach allows users to focus on the most critical areas of inefficiency, accelerating the path to resolution.

 

Benefits of Using Filters in Process Insights

Enhanced Root Cause Analysis

By narrowing down issues using What, Where, and When, businesses can dive deeper into the specific causes of inefficiencies, whether they stem from system errors, resource constraints, or procedural gaps.

Improved Decision-Making

Filters enable users to transform raw data into actionable insights, helping stakeholders prioritize and implement changes where they matter most.

Cost Savings and Efficiency Gains

By focusing efforts on high-impact areas, businesses can streamline operations, reduce waste, and enhance productivity.

Conclusion

SAP Signavio Process Insights, discovery edition, offers an intuitive and powerful way to analyze process performance through its What, Where, When filters. These tools empower businesses to uncover inefficiencies, conduct detailed root cause analysis, and make informed decisions to drive continuous improvement.

With these filters in hand, businesses can move beyond high-level observations to take targeted, data-driven actions that improve their processes and position them for long-term success.

Stay tuned for more insights on how SAP Signavio Process Insights can transform your process management journey.

Hi 

For External Service Procurement Please go through below 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/af9ef57f504840d2b81be8667206d485/1403b953495bb44ce10000000a174cb4.html

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/lean-service-procurement-in-sap-s-4hana/ba-p/13488178

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-lean-services-in-procurement-transforming-from-complex-to-collaborative/ba-p/13535527

To decide which one, here we need to analyse the requirement so It's up to  business requirements

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Re: Error when reading a released CDS view from a release...
entry.content: try adding this "WITH PRIVILEGED ACCESS" to the select

entry.title: Easy Expert functionality does not contain specific characteristic of a Value Assignment
entry.content:
We are looking to add GB Validity Area to existing instances based on certain Value Assignments as instructed by our Product Safety and Regulatory Affairs dept. I plan to utilize Easy Expert functionality to do this.

However on checking the functionality, I've encountered a problem to one specific Value Assignment: Hazard Ingredients.

I accessed Easy Expert function via MORE > UTILITIES > SETS OF RULES > NEW while in t/code CG02 when in a spec.

In the Value Assignment section, I selected Change Existing Value Assignment and enter into the Multiple Selection button to specify my requirements.

Specifically Hazard Ingredients, the only field listed is REMARKS on the Easy Expert Characteristic Changes box.

Is there a way to include other Characteristics to a specific Value Assignment currently not present within the Easy Expert function, please?

I plan to include an additional GB Validity entry to existing instances but only based on specific Hazard Comp rule entry that appears in SOURCE NOTE characteristic.

(attachments included)

We need to have this option otherwise it will have a negative impact on our data load plans on our live system.

entry.title: Can't create STO between two storage location within the same plant
entry.content:
Can't create STO between two storage location within the same plant


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Can't create STO between two storage location within...
entry.content:
What you are seeing here is correct (from the system logic perspective) - 

> STO is for shipping goods "between" two points - plant-to-plant, so an STO will not work here, as the receiving / accepting plants are one and the same; 

> For movements in the same plant, the STO is not needed - any regular stock movements (eg MIGO with Movement 101 / 102 / etc) will work here instead. 

Thanks for the question!

entry.title: Re: How do I assign BRF+ 'configuration' in the MDG Conso...
entry.content: Thank you Christina. I've read through all of the documentation for both the Update and Derivation steps, and this is how I understood it as well. Just so I'm clear, there is nothing to be populated in the configuration dropdown for the Update or Derivation steps, as I've configured them to both use BRF+. The 'rules' we build out under the relevant function(s) in BRF+ will automatically be called?

entry.title: Re: Webservice call error from S4HANA/ECC to BTP CPI
entry.content: Usually it is sufficient, to import the CA root certificates into the "trust store" of the other side. So the CA root, which signed the CPI's server certificate, needs to be imported into the trust list of the SAP backend (transaction STRUST), and the CA root, which signed the SAP backend's client certificate, needs to be imported into the CPI's trust store.

Can you update this excel with the columns unprotected?

Thanks

entry.title: Defect Management with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
This article describes how to use the Defect Management feature of SAP Cloud ALM (CALM) from tester, defect processor and test management point of view while explaining the most important new features and outlining differences to the process in SAP Solution Manager.

 

Prerequisites

While it's possible to simply create a stand-alone defect using the Defect Management App, we will assume the most common use case - which is defects being created from a test run of a test cases belonging to a test plan. For a quick overview on how test plans, test cases and runs work in Cloud ALM kindly refer to this post.  

 

Defect Creation (Tester point of view)

In most cases the tester will create a new defect from a test run after setting status of the corresponding test step to "Fail" using the corresponding button:

Next, enter the details and ideally including screenshots (can be conveniently pasted from the clipboard into the description):

Note the link to the test case is automatically populated. All details of the defect can be maintained already at creation, including defect status and the defect processor (Field "Responsible"). 
If the tester is not able (or not supposed) to put in the responsible right away you can ask you testers to always assign a dedicated dispatcher as responsible or use the "Team" field. The fields "Status", "Responsible" and "Team" can be found in the very bottom / tab "Additional Information". 

 

Notifications

Upon being assigned and on status change of the defect, the "Responsible" will by default get a notification within Cloud ALM:

E-Mail notification can be turned on in your notification settings:

Note: To be able to make this setting for defects, a defect must be assigned to you. Otherwise the setting will not show. Also this setting must be made by each user individually.  

 

Defect Processing (Processor point of view)

The "Responsible" will likely access the defect from a CALM notification, the link included in the e-mail notification or by filtering for him/herself in the "Defects" app: 

The "Responsible" will the change the status to "In Progress", indicating he/she has begun working on the defect. You can switch from/to any status by selecting it from the dropdown box.


It's worth pointing out that status "Closed" does not lock the defect for good, like setting a final status for a defect in Solution manager would. So you can (at least at the time of writing) still go back from "Closed" to any other status.

Sub-tasks

If the resolution of the defects requires steps from more than just one party, subtasks can be defined - each one with a responsible of its own (and yes, notifications also work the same way for subtasks).

Once Subtasks are defined, the degree of completion will also be displayed as a progress bar (see screenshots further below).

Exchanging information between parties involved in defect resolution

Unlike with classic solution manager defects the field "Responsible" is to be updated each time the status changes to the party that has to take the next action. The notifications will always go to the "Responsible".

In classic solution manager defects -depending on the status- a different party had to take action and -if configured- was notified by mail (Example: Status new --> next action by processor; Status solution proposal --> next action by tester / creator).

Thus if the defect is still in process and the "Responsible" has a question to the tester/Creator  he/she will just leave the defect in status "In Progress" and change the responsible back to the tester/creator (and ideally add the question as a comment).

The question can either be added to the description (where we also put the screenshot) or appended as a comment.
While changes to the description are instantly visible, comments requires an extra click to be displayed (thus there's a small chance for comments to be missed by untrained test participants):

I would still recommend to use comments, as these also log author and timestamp and are displayed ordered by the latter - so conversations between the parties involved in defect resolution are much easier to keep track of:

 

How to transport fixes / corrections?

While there are no dedicated defect corrections (as you may know from SAP Solution Manager + Focused Build) in CALM "Features" can be linked to defects by reference and used to transport fixes through the system landscape.
Please refer to the post  Embrace Defect Corrections with SAP Cloud ALM by colleague @mariiakonikova for how it can be done. 

 

Other features (History / Copy / Share by mail) 

Before you ask: Yes, the defect comes with a history in which all changes are logged including timestamp and  the corresponding user:

Need to notify somebody of a defect but d not want to change the responsible? The "Share by Mail" functionality will open a template email with a deep link to the defect, ready to be sent:

 

Unlike in Solution Manager, Cloud ALM allows defects to be copied. You can chose whether you want to also copy Sub-tasks: 

Just like when opening a defect, all fields and sub-items can be adjusted before saving the copy. Keep in mind though, that test case assignments are not copied - so you will have to assign a newly copied defect to a test case / plan manually.

 

Reporting  (Test manager point of view)

Assuming the most common scenario: If you want to get an overview about open defects for a running test cycle as a test manager, the "Defects" app is the place to start. Here you can filter for the test plan representing your test cycle and use the status filter to only show defects that are not already closed. Filter Criteria can be adjusted using the "Adapt Filters" button, columns in the results area through the "Settings" button (gear symbol).
You can also export the results to excel including al the details per defect using the download button (down arrow symbol):

 

If you want to report on how many defects you have in which status and how the resolution is progressing click the "Analytics" button in the top right. This will take you to the Defect Reporting section of CLAM's Analytics module:

Here you have 3 views: 
Defect Distribution:
Check how many defects you have in which status. You can also report across several test plans and group by test plan.

Created vs. Resolved:
Show how many defects were created /resolved during the last x days. (If there is an imbalance towards new defects created, this may indicate a capacity issue in defect processing.)

History:
Defect distribution over time.

 

Summary & closing thoughts

Quite a few useful (some might say essential) features have been added since Cloud ALM's defect management has been released about 2 years ago.
Still, keep in mind this article is merely a snapshot of what Cloud ALM can do at the time of writing. With new features being rolled out on a bi-weekly schedule this article may already be outdated by the time of reading:

Which Cloud ALM features have ben added recently? LINK
Which Cloud ALM features are in the pipeline? LINK
Want to try Cloud ALM for yourself? Try the Public Demo System.

entry.title: Re: New in Production Accounting – Event-Based Production Cost Posting
entry.content:
Hi @EvaWang @ShugeGuo @olivia_wang 

What is the way to adjust the overhead calculation on an already posted Event-based prod order ? The overhead rate might have been maintained incorrect or actual OH rate is determined only at the period end

Is Actual Costing the only way to handle this ? Does actual costing in UPA revaluate for both actual OH rates and actual activity prices ? Without UPA, it only revaluates for actual activity prices

entry.title: Re: Upgrade Mobile Application Integration Framework (MAIF) using SAP Note Transport-based Correctio
entry.content:
Hi Dirk,

    Like any other S/4 upgrade, with new FPS/SPS package which is higher than the TCI note validity, the latest code lines will be imported which may differ from what you imported with TCI and those code lines will be updated. You may need to adjust certain objects using SPAU/SPDD process during the upgrade. 

But it will not impact your current SSAM configuration, we highly recommend testing SSAM application after the upgrade to identify any issues with the upgrade based on your current configuration and set-up. 

Thanks

Syam

entry.title: Re: How to add new attachment to existing equipment new mass
entry.content:
Hi @AndreasMuno, Thanks for your response and apologies for the inconvenience. 

Business wanted an automatically attach a common PDF (Blank Work Permit Document) for all the Maintenance orders generated and that needs to be print along with maintenance order.

So, that a PDF attached to the technical object will be automatically available in the maintenance orders created or generated from the maintenance plan and then by enabling the automatic attachment printing option, an attachment can be print along with the maintenance order.

To execute this, we need to attach a common work permit document to all the equipment's. So, my question is like "Can we able to attach a same PDF document to all the available equipment's in mass?"

I hope, it is clear now, could you please help us with the details.

 

Regards,

Yaswanth.

entry.title: SAP ALM Roadshow 2025 - Mexico Edition!
entry.content:
Estimado cliente,
¡Tenemos algunas noticias emocionantes que compartir! Después de exitosos road shows en São Paulo y Buenos Aires en noviembre de 2024, estamos encantados de invitarlo a SAP Cloud ALM Roadshow LAC en Monterrey, México, el 29 de enero de 2025.

Este evento es una oportunidad única para conocer las últimas innovaciones en SAP Cloud Application Lifecycle Management (Cloud ALM) y descubrir herramientas y estrategias que pueden ayudarlo a optimizar sus proyectos.

Por qué no deberías perderte este evento:
- Información estratégica exclusiva: Obtenga una comprensión profunda de las últimas innovaciones y estrategias en SAP Cloud ALM.
- Soluciones específicas de la región: conozca cómo SAP Cloud ALM aborda los desafíos únicos a los que se enfrentan las empresas de América Latina.
- Networking: conéctese con expertos de SAP, pares y líderes de la industria de la región.

Temas clave:
- SAP Cloud ALM para RISE with SAP y estrategias de soporte core limpio
- Implementación de empresa inteligente con SAP Cloud ALM
- Automatización de pruebas con Tricentis
- Optimización operativa combinando IA y SAP Cloud ALM
- Aprovechamiento de SAP Focused Run para las capacidades de supervisión avanzada
- Transición de SAP Solution Manager a SAP Cloud ALM
- [Opcional] Badge de Conocimiento de transformación 2024 - Consultor de transformación de soluciones: mejore su experiencia realizando la prueba opcional que lo ayudarán a comprender la estrategia de ALM y sus capacidades. Más información (la prueba se realizará en inglés)

Detalles de evento:
- Fecha: 29 de enero de 2025
- Ubicación: Ave. Ricardo Margain 335, Torre 1 - Piso 16; Col. Valle del Campestre, San Pedro Garza García, N.L., México - CP 66265
- Hora: 10:00 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. (GMT-3)

¡Regístrese ahora! 

¡Los espacios son limitados, así que asegure su lugar hoy!

Manténgase atento para obtener más actualizaciones, anuncios de oradores y agendas detalladas para el evento. ¡Nos vemos en México! 

En caso de duda, póngase en contacto con sapalmus@sap.com

#SAPCloudALM #ALM #ALMSummit #SAPSummit #SAPEvents #SolmanTransition

entry.title: Re: Enabling Identity Authentication integration in LMS
entry.content: @MaoAcevedo, can you check if the following documentation section helps you: Learning Configuration Procedure?

entry.title: Re: SAP PI REST-Sender Adapter Custom Error Handling Expr...
entry.content:
The solution is:
I have changed the error handling from JSON expression to XPATH expression
and entered XPATH /ns0:errorReply/ns0:errorReply/ns0:status and query for 400 or 404
--> worked, at 200 it stays 200, at 500 from the backend with payload status = 400 it becomes 400, with status = 404 it becomes 404

Important is the right XPATH with namespace



It would be interesting to know why JSON Expression somehow starts up and yet doesn't work right, or what it is actually used for.
I tried XPATH Expression once, by the way, but made a typo 🙈 and didn't pursue it any further.

entry.title: Several customers reporting that they haven't received emails that were sent from C4C
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

We have several reports from our customers that for a number of emails sent from us from within c4c "that they have not recived an answer from us". We don't currently know whether these emails actually went into the customers spam/junk or if they were not delivered at all.

I have checked :

  1. DKIM and DMARC were already in place at the time that the emails were sent.
  2. DKIM/DMARC/SPF currently pass.
  3. On the case/ticket there is no indication that the email was "bounced" and the bounced indicator in activies->emails = false.
  4. I have tested sending emails to my email from the same channel in c4c and all received without issue.

Has anyone else had experience of this and if so, what was the cause and/or resolution.  Thanks,

Samir

(fyi I have also reported to SAP via incident in parallel with full details of a specific example for them to check the delivery, but from my experience that will usually take a lot of hard work and time and I may not get a meaningful response from them in the end so asking here in parallel).

entry.title: Re: Webservice call error from S4HANA/ECC to BTP CPI
entry.content:
Only the root and the intermediate certificates are now required to be imported into the SSL client view of STRUST. All three used to be required, but recent changes to how the load-balancing is handled led to only the two being required. On the CPI side you need to import the SAP system certificate, but it must be provided by one of the approved CAs.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Query: Best Practices for Workflow Email Notifications to Shared Mailbox
entry.content:
Hi,

Our admin team has a requirement that all workflow email notifications, where they are part of the CC role, should be sent to a specific shared mailbox. This mailbox is already available in Active Directory. Currently, we have created a workflow group called 'XYZ' (as an example) and added the admin team members to this group. The group is then assigned the CC role in the workflow, resulting in all four or five members receiving individual emails.

I am considering creating one generic account in EC called 'XYZ,' assigning the shared mailbox email ID (available in Active Directory) to this account, and granting the workflow permission to the 'XYZ' account. However, I am unsure if this approach aligns with best practices or if there are any data privacy or security risks involved. Could you please advise?

entry.title: Bp replication based on supplier governance
entry.content:
Hi all,

Our client has implemented supplier,customer governance which inturn creates BPs also.

Now the requirement is to replicate BPs to another SAP system, my question is since we are using customer, supplier CRS in stead of BP cr type, is it possible to use SOA based replication? If yes pls share the steps to be followed on receiving system.

Thanks in advance..

Kumar.

entry.title: Re: Tips for Using Concurrent Employment in SuccessFactors Employee Central
entry.content:
Hi, we have concurrent employee for two diferents legal entities from diferents countries and diferents payroll groups. So, the BIB integration integrates based on the primary employment, checks the payroll group from this, even dought this employment is inactive. 

Is there any system parameter to config that the BIB checks the payroll areas from de active employement? or any other way to integrate this cases? As the payroll area are from different countries is very complex to integrate a concurrent with this scenario. 

Thanks at all 

Regards

entry.title: Payment block substitution Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello! 

i need to substitute (populate) the payment block while creating the Invoice for specific conditions.

As i can see BADI FIN_ACDOC_ITEM_SUBSTITUTION doesn't have this field for substitution.

How can i achieve it in the Public cloud? can i add standard field to the list of target fields? or maybe there is another way to populate the payment block to the document?

Thank you!

entry.title: Hi Experts,Generally how much time it takes to restore deleted BTP hana instance.
Re: SAP GUI JAVA version 8 or higher not able to download
entry.content: Make sure you're in "Support Packages & Patches" in the Download Software section, not "Installations & Upgrades".

entry.title: Re: Unable to purchase SAP certification exam
entry.content: With screenshots:

entry.title: Is SQL Anywhere viable and sustainable
entry.content:
I have a consult that is telling us we need to move away from SQL Anywhere, now and immediately, for the following reasons:

  • It is End of Life (from what I can tell the current version is EOL in 2028)
  • Limited interoperability
  • There are many more modern database solutions available for YEARS that would better position us for growth and modernization
  • SQLA is not a modern technology
  • Other solutions provide improved scalability and performance as SQLA is not designed for large-scale applications
  • Other solutions provide enhanced security and compliance
  • Other solutions offer great integration and flexibility, having extensive integration capabilities with various tools and platforms, including cloud services and container orchestration systems like Kubernetes.
  • Other solutions have long-term viability and ecosystem support from strong communities and corporations.

Are they speaking truth? half-truths?

We use SQLA in a highly available 3-node cluster in a single corporate server/data center.

entry.title: LSMW issue for transaction CG02BD
entry.content: While using LSMW to remove reference number and material from the substance for transaction CG02BD, I am getting screen field errors that particular screen fields are not available. Please let me know if there are any inputs.

entry.title: Looking for advanced training material and/or classes
entry.content: We're looking for advanced training material and or classes for using SAP Cloud Application Studio to enhance our instance of SAP Business ByDesign. For instance, there's only one tutorial series under learning.sap.com that touches on the UI Designer and it's very basic.

entry.title: Not receiving Digital signature emails into C4C
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

We have enabled SMIME settings(Open activity config- E-Mail Encryption and Signature Check) in C4C to allow digital signature emails only from customers. 

After activating the above settings, still emails sent by the customers are blocked by SAP and they are getting email notification 'Signature verification failed'.

Couple of questions:

1. Though we activated the SMIME settings why SAP is blocking those emails?

2. Do we need to share the SAP CA certificate maintained in open activity config 'Load Certificates and Activate Signing and Encryption for E-Mails' to the customers? If that is the case do they need to upload the certificate in their email server before they send digital signature emails? or 

3. Do we need to upload the SAP CA certificate in our internal O365 servers as the emails are forwarded from our internal mail servers?

4. Any other settings that we are missing except the SMIME settings in two open activity configurations 'E-Mail Encryption and Signature Check' & 'Load Certificates and Activate Signing and Encryption for E-Mails'.

I am sure you might have came across this scenario, please let me know your thoughts and possible solution.

entry.title: Re: S4HC public 2308 > eDocument for Romania not created
entry.content:
Kindly refer here for the updated information -

Romania | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: SuccessFactor Learning, e-signature comment
entry.content:
Since version 2405p19, SAP has instroduced a new visual for the learning History. 

Did anyone notice that since then we can't see the e-signature comment of the user completing the training anymore.

This comment is shown when looking at the details of the Items from the Administrator's View, but NOT from the User's view.

P.S. e-signature comment from an additional approval are shown.

entry.title: Re: Which SAP Data Services( BODS) 4.3 versions supports...

is RHEL 8.10 is also supported for SAP DATA services 4.3 SP02 patch 4?
is RHEL 8.10 is supported for SAP DATA services 4.2 SP 14 patch 23 as per the https://support.sap.com/content/dam/launchpad/en_us/pam/pam-essentials/TIP/PAM_DataServices_4_2.pdf page 8?

entry.title: Re: Issue with Employee History Salary Field for Hourly P...
entry.content: @MariaCarlotaAbreu @gregwilliams   I'm sure at this time you've received an update from SAP support on the incident(s) you created but in case other partners/customers find this thread : this issue is a Product issue that our Product team is looking at patching asap.  Jira : PTCH-45731 for reference in communication with SAP support teams to know the latest date the patch is planned for.  Currently planned for Dec 30, 2024.

entry.title: Re: Which SAP Data Services( BODS) 4.3 versions supports RHEL 8.6 or 8.9 ?
entry.content: is RHEL 8.10 is also supported for SAP DATA services 4.3 SP02 patch 4

entry.title: Re: Posting only possible in periods 2024/12 and 2024/10...
entry.content:
Hi, 

If MMPV/RV is already open, ensure the FI periods are also open.

Check OB52 with your Posting period variant.

VG

AS

entry.title: Memory issue in SAP application server
entry.content:
The work processes at the application level are in a waiting state, but at the OS level, they are still holding between 30 to 40 GB of memory, which is not being released and causing memory issues.

Please advise on any parameters or configurations that can help restrict memory allocation and facilitate the release of memory in such cases.

entry.title: We have DUMP after applying NFE 4.0 notes
entry.content:
We are applying notes in the environment, updating to NFe 4.0.

We encountered this DUMP, is there a correction note available?

How can we resolve this?

Best regards!

entry.title: Re: #COE #BA01 Notifying users when Supplier Invoice work...
entry.content: Hi Daisy - Appreciate the response. As mentioned above we're not going to be giving an administration app (and the other apps found in that catalog) to end users. By their nature and business role template assignment, apps like Flexible Workflow Administration are intended to system administrator use not general users like the AP Coordinators. Is there no other way to get this data? Seems like a pretty big miss that documents with errors aren't filterable/searchable within the AP applications and the workflow doesn't address error communication.

entry.title: Re: Regarding updating BLOB in BTP Cloud Hana DB via CAPM...
entry.content: Hello David, Good day!! Thanks a lot for your response. We are following the same and the service works as expected, but when we try to code the same in a UI5 app controller this is not working as expected. Is there any sample or example code snippet for handling this scenario in UI5 app?

entry.title: Re: SWPM 1.0 SP28 linux software.

entry.title: Re: SWPM 1.0 SP28 linux software.
entry.content: Hi, What do you need? What do you want to do?

entry.title: Re: SWPM 1.0 SP28 linux software.
entry.content: I want the SWPM 1.0 SP28 linux software, I want to do system refresh using export and import, so If you have a SWPM-SP28 linux version, could you please send me.

entry.title: Re: Memory issue in SAP application server
entry.content: Hello, SAP system manages memory itself. Memory is reserved the moment the system. 88416 - Zero administration memory management for the ABAP server is opened. If the application and database are on the same server, you can review the zero memory management note.

entry.title: Re: Proration based upon leaves type in variable pay temp...
entry.content: Hi @Ritanshi , you can point your main record rule (the one at the bottom of EC Settings page) to include all events but "Unpaid Leaves" but you cannot build it based on Event Reasons in a reliable way. If you base it on Event reasons then you run the risk of another event being added and preventing data accuracy on proration. Which is why I would recommend adding an extra field to Job Info and using that field in your rule. Thanks, Xavier

entry.title: Re: Enabling Identity Authentication integration in LMS
entry.content: Good afternoon team, I appreciate the information shared, likewise, the concern was regarding which is the best alternative for the default user source system, taking into account that the client has both internal and external user types, which according to the indications in the note https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_LEARNING/82cf7c83c7db42a8aa1d3bbdbc39e93d/3f29058a9dfe4ce38a3c774fbdf5339b.html?locale=en-US , a learning-only user or a platform user must be chosen. 2. Within the indications of the shared note, it is not clear how those external users created in LMS will reach IAS. I am attentive to your comments. Cordially,

entry.title: Re: SWPM 1.0 SP28 linux software.
entry.content:
Hi,

There is a download link in the note below, you can download it with your sap user.

1680045 - Release Note for Software Provisioning Manager 1.0 (recommended: SWPM 1.0)

entry.title: Re: IDES ECC6 EHP8 working - ADS adobe / JAVA STACK issue
entry.content:
Hello, This topic is a bit tricky. Unfortunately, you cannot run ADS without a Java application server. First, you need to install NW 7.5 and then configure ADS. If you are not an expert, this process can be long and complicated.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-netweaver-7-5-installation-on-oracle-19c/ba-p/13437211

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/ads-configuration-in-netweaver-7-5/ba-p/13303580

entry.title: Re: SWPM 1.0 SP28 linux software.

entry.title: C4C and Twilio Integration
entry.content:
Hello,

Has anyone integrated C4C with Twilio for SMS functionality? If so, could you please share some insights on this? Additionally, I would like to see how the SMS integration UI appears in C4C for both inbound and outbound messages.

entry.title: Re: SWPM 1.0 SP28 linux software.
entry.content: Unfortunately, I don't have permission to download it either. Old SWPM (Software Provisioning Manager) versions are typically archived and may not be directly accessible due to SAP's "End of Maintenance" policies. In such cases, you can contact SAP Customer Support to request a specific version. Who to contact... If issues persist after performing the above, create a case for XX-SER-FORME and detail which items you performed (above). It is helpful to include your S-user ID and Password (or UID password) in the secure area of the case according to KBA 1773689 so SAP can simulate the download issue.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Integration with Board
entry.content: Board can connect to the HANA Cloud of Datasphere via ODBC: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/be5967d099974c69b77f4549425ca4c0/3de55a78a4614deda589633baea28645.html

entry.title: FAQs regrading Receiving process in Ariba
entry.content:
Receiving is an essential function in the P2P process, both from the audit and compliance perspective.

The act of receiving services or goods to confirm that the goods or services ordered from a supplier have been delivered and are satisfactory.

Receiving ensures that the enterprise pays the suppliers correctly based on the actual deliveries received. Receiving also provides a mechanism for returning undesired items. Specific scenarios, such as Asset receiving, require additional data entry during the receiving process.

 

Let's dive deep into the Receiving function and understand it using FAQs

Receiving Methods

Q - Who can do Receiving, and where should the receiving information be captured?A - Receiving can be one in Ariba and/or external systems such as ERP. Please see below the receiving methods available in Ariba.

  1. Automatic receiving—This method enables Ariba to create receipts automatically based on specific configurations.
  2. Manual Receiving
    1. In this method, the users manually create a receipt that indicates the extent to which the order was received. Users can also revise the quantity or amount downward by entering negative values, as you might reject some items. 
    2. Manual Receiving Types
      1. Desktop receiving—In this type, the Casual user (who must have the Purchasing User group assigned) receives the goods.
      2. Central Receiving – Receiving is done by the team/member who must have a Receiving Manager or Receiving Agent group assigned in Ariba
  3. Line-level receiving: In this method, receipts are created in the ERP system and imported into Ariba as proof of records.

Manual Receiving Method -

Q - Can I receive multiple orders simultaneously when manually receiving them?

A - You can receive multiple orders simultaneously, provided the orders don't require additional data entry.

However, If any order requires asset or other data entry in the receipt, you're notified to enter individual receipts for those items.

Q - Can I create multiple receipts for a PO?

Yes, you can do partial receiving. If the order is partially received and remains open, you must create additional receipts as you receive additional line items.

Q - Can I create one Receipt for multiple PO in Ariba?

While partial receiving, which is multiple receipts to fulfill a PO, is supported in Ariba, a single receipt for multiple purchase orders isn't.

Q - WHAT are the different types of manual receipts that can be done in Ariba?

Below are the Receiving Types available in Ariba

  1. Manual Receive by Quantity
    1. Manually Receive by item quantity
    2. This is the Default receiving type
  2. Manual Receive by Amount
    1. Manually Receive by dollar amount

Q - How do we determine the correct receiving type?

Organizations generally use quantity-based receiving for goods items and amount-based receiving for service items. 

Q - Do you have a workflow diagram for Manual Receiving?

Automatic Receiving Method -

Q - How do I decide if my company should go for automatic receiving or receive the goods manually?

A - The below use cases drive the automatic receiving of goods -

  1. To eliminate time spent on data entry for low-value receipts
  2. To reduce the risk of pending payments for small invoices awaiting a manual receipt.

Q - Can I do manual receiving for a commodity marked as auto-receive to make corrections?

A - Yes, you can. When you manually receive a line item initially configured for auto-receiving, Ariba converts the receiving type for that line to a manual receipt type.

Q - Can I change the purchase order or cancel it entirely after it's auto-received?

A—Yes, you can change/cancel an auto-received PO. To do so, you need to create negative receipts for the PO, thereby converting it to a manually received PO. Later, you can choose to either change or cancel such purchase orders.

Q - What are the different ways I can do automatic receiving in Ariba?

A - Receipts are automatically created based on auto-receiving configuration.

Ariba offers the below receiving types options -

  1. Auto Receive below a specific dollar amount: Items of lower dollar value will be auto-received. If the receipt value exceeds the specific dollar amount, someone must manually receive the item.
  2. Auto-receipt on due date: Generates a receipt on the order due date.
  3. Auto-receipt on order date: Generates a receipt when the item is ordered.
  4. Auto-receipt on invoice reconciliation: Generates a receipt when the invoice reconciliation document is generated.
  5. Auto-receipt on charge reconciliation: For PCard orders, a receipt when the charge reconciliation document is generated.
  6. Auto-receipt on invoice or charge reconciliation: This function generates a receipt when either an invoice reconciliation document or a charge reconciliation document is generated.

Q - How do I know when my order is due for automatic receiving?

A - Auto-receipt on the due date is the ideal configuration for receiving catalog items automatically, where the due date is calculated based on catalog item lead time. For non-catalog items, the default lead time parameter is zero in Ariba.

Line-level Receiving Method -

Q - Can I receive goods partially in Ariba and ERP?

A - Line-level receiving method in Ariba enables users to receive items in ERP/Ariba. Receipts can be handled for a PO using a combination of manual and imported receipts. Ariba offers below options, which enterprises can choose depending on the business rule and the use case

  1. line-level receiving in the enhanced mode -
    1. Users can create receipts from the buying solution by importing receipt data from the external ERP systems.
    2. The Application.Receiving.AllowReceivingByUserInterfaceAndReceiptImportEnhanced parameter (set by SAP Ariba) must be enabled to create receipts for a single line item on order in multiple ways.
  2. line-level receiving, without the enhanced mode -
    1. create receipts for a single line item on an order either in the buying solution or by using imported receipts, but not both.
    2. A member of the Customer Administrator group sets this parameter in the ICM - Application.Receiving.AllowReceivingByUserInterfaceAndReceiptImport ;
      1. When set to Yes, receivers can create receipts either in the buying solution or by using imported receipts, but not both.
      2. When set to No, the way receivers can create receipts depends on how the first receiving transaction is processed.

Q - How do I configure my organization's Receiving process in Ariba ?

Examples of Receiving configuration options available in Ariba include:

  1. Auto-receiving can be enabled based on the Po line item's supplier part number or commodity code.
  2. Threshold amounts for specific commodities and supplier part numbers are configured in Ariba so that the items are auto-received only if the order line is under the specified amount.
  3. Ariba also offers flexibility in determining the Receiving types based on specific fields in the requisition, including custom fields.
  4. Tolerance settings are used to control over- and under-receiving orders at the customer site in general and at specific purchasing unit levels.

Q—How can I automatically make a category receive low values while manually receiving high values for the same category?

Amount thresholds determine whether POs are auto-received or manually received based on the amount of the PO line item. The buying solution automatically receives PO lines with an amount less than or equal to the threshold amount.

There are two ways to configure amount thresholds for auto-receiving:

  1. You can configure auto-receiving amount thresholds for order lines based on commodity code or supplier part number. This way, the amount threshold for one commodity code or part number can be different from the threshold for another commodity code or part number.
  2. SAP Ariba Customer Support can configure site-wide amount thresholds at the order and line levels. These site-wide thresholds apply to all lines and orders, regardless of commodity code or part number.

Q—I want to set the receiving type for a commodity code as manual; however, I want to configure auto-receiving for a particular part number belonging to that commodity code. Is it possible?

A—We understand that such business requirements are often required. Hence, Ariba offers this functionality: for a particular item, the receiving type associated with the part number takes precedence over the receiving type associated with the commodity code.

Under & Over Receiving

Q - Can I receive more or less than the quantity/amount ordered?

Yes, Buyers can receive more or less than the ordered quantity, depending on business needs. Ariba provides the under-receiving and over-receiving functionality.

Q - Why do you want to under/over-receive?

In general, the quantities and amounts on a receipt are expected to match those on the purchase order. However, sometimes you may receive fewer items or for a lesser amount than what was specified on the purchase order (under-receiving), or you may receive more items or for a higher amount than on the purchase order (over-receiving).

Q - Is there a limit to how much I can under/over-receive?

Yes, such limits are governed by the receiving tolerance configured in Ariba. The purchase order will be considered fully received if you submit receipts for quantities and/or amounts within the configured tolerance limits.

Tolerance can be defined based on -

  1. Absolute Quantity
  2. Value (Line Item Amount)
  3. Percentage of Qty/Amount

Q: Do I need to meet all the above-listed tolerances for receiving, or is meeting anyone’s tolerance enough?

Your site can be configured so that absolute and relative tolerances must be met for a receipt to be fully received, or either one of the tolerance settings must be met for the receipt to be complete.

Please note that all three attributes must be met in the default configuration. If you want to change this operator, have your DSC log a case.

Let's understand this default configuration with two examples: manual receipt by quantity and manual receipt by Amount.

Default Configuration - All three attributes must be met for a receipt to be fully received;

 Quantity tolerance setting: 5,

Percentage tolerance setting: 2%,

Value tolerance setting: $50

The purchase order for Part A is for a quantity of 100. The item price is $20. Receiving is configured for manual receipt by quantity:

  1. Example A - Under-receiving when the quantity does not meet the tolerance limit:
    1. Receive by quantity: 96.
    2. The order has yet to be fully received and will remain received since it did not meet the 2% limit (under-receiving up to a quantity of 2 for this order).
  1. Example B - Under-receiving when the quantity is below the tolerance limit:
    1. Receive by quantity: 99.
    2. Tolerance limits are met, and the order is considered fully received and can be closed.

The purchase order for Part B is for a quantity of 200. The item price is $15. Receiving for PartB is configured for manual receipt by amount:

  1. Example C - Over-receiving when the amount is within the tolerance limit: Receive by amount: $3,050. Tolerance limits are met for percentage and value limits ($60), and the order is considered fully received.
  2. Example D - Under-receiving when the amount does not meet the tolerance limit: Receive by amount: $2,940. The tolerance limit for percentage is met, but the tolerance limit for value is not, and the order still needs to be fully received. The order remains Receiving.

Q—So far, so good; however, in a complex business scenario where a company has many subsidiaries that function as independent purchasing units, the receiving behavior for such subsidiaries may differ from that of the parent company due to various business requirements. How do we deal with such a difference in receiving tolerance requirements?

A - To facilitate such differences at the purchasing unit level, you can now configure the receiving tolerances specific to each purchasing unit. One thing you need to keep in mind is that Purchasing unit hierarchies for receiving tolerances are not supported. Hence, please configure the tolerance for each PU; otherwise, the receiving tolerances that are configured at your customer site level will be applicable for such unconfigured purchasing units.

Receipt Information Sent to Suppliers

Q - How do I inform my suppliers that I have received the items?

A—You can configure your buying solution to send receipt information to SAP Business Network, allowing suppliers to view the approval status of receipts and receipt information, such as the number of items accepted or rejected.

A member of the Customer Administrator group sets this parameter in ICM - Application.Receiving.SendReceiptsToAribaNetwork

Q - Why should I send receipt information to my suppliers?

A—With receipt information available on SAP Business Network, suppliers can view the status of receipt transactions and create invoices based on actual receipts. This enables quicker transaction closure and reduces receipt-related exceptions while reconciling invoices.

Asset Data on Receipts

Q - Can I receive Assets in Ariba?

A - Yes, Ariba supports Asset-receiving functionality. Asset receiving in Ariba allows asset data (for example, asset serial number and tag number) to be added to the receipt line items identified as assets.

Q - How do I configure specific categories/part # as Assets in Ariba?

A - A receipt line item is identified as an Asset based on the Part Number or Commodity Code. If the asset data feature is enabled on your site, adding asset details to the corresponding asset line items in the receipt is mandatory before it is approved.

 Enabling the asset data feature helps ensure that information on a receipt is accurate before approval.

 

You can add asset data in one of the following ways (Help link included) :

  1. Manually, for each received item – The procedure is described here. Specifying Asset Data Manually for Received Items
  2. By importing asset data from an Excel template. The procedure is described here: Specifying Asset Data Using an Excel Template.
  3. Automatically based on the value set in the Application.Procure.DefaultAssetDataSize (Set by SAP Ariba Support) parameter. Details can be seen here -  DefaultAssetDataSize.

Q - Who can add the asset data to the receipts before they are sent to the ERP for processing?

Either the requester or any one of the approvers must ensure that asset data is specified before the receipts are fully approved and sent to the ERP.

Typically, the main receiver adds asset data at the time of receiving, but this data may not always be accurate.

To ensure accurate information is provided, you can add Asset Receiver group users to the approval flow.

The asset receiver can then review the specified asset information, change it if necessary, and approve the receipt.

Additional Information :

In the default configuration, when asset data is enabled for receiving, an asset receiver or the customer administrator is automatically added at the end of the receipt approval process. This ensures that asset data is specified before the receipt is fully approved.

An exception is raised if the asset receiver tries to approve the receipt without adding this asset data.

System Configuration

The 4-step process of configuring Receiving in Ariba

  1. Enable Receiving in Ariba using site Parameter
  2. Configuring Receiving by Commodity, part#, etc.
  3. Add 'Receivers' using Approval groups
  4. Configure Receipt integration as required

Q - How can I add Receivers in Ariba to make them responsible for receiving?

A - Designating the "Receiver" in Ariba can be done using the Approval process

  • The person or Group responsible for  doing the receiving against a Purchase Order (manual receiving) is designated through a Receipt "Approval" ruleset
  • The first "Approver" in the ruleset is the  person who needs to do the receiving (complete the required fields to submit the Receipt)
  • The Receipt "Approvals" have the same capabilities as another approval rule, including leveraging Lookup Tables.
  • It is not common to require additional Receipt approvals other than for asset-receiving

Q - How can I map receiving types with commodity codes and amount thresholds?

The Import Receiving Types By Commodity Code data import task lets you associate receiving types with commodity codes and amount thresholds.

Here, you can also specify which commodity codes require asset data information.

If necessary, you can also configure custom conditions to determine the receiving type based on the fields on the requisition.

Import Receiving Types By Commodity Code reads data from the CSV file ReceiptByCommodityCode.csv.

Q - How can I map receiving types with Part numbers?

The Import Receiving Types By Part Number data import task associates receiving types and amount thresholds with part numbers. It also specifies which part numbers require asset data information. Import Receiving Types By Part Number reads data from the CSV file named ReceiptByPartNumber.csv.

Thank you for taking the time to read the blog. I hope it helps you build a business case for choosing Ariba for your receiving process. If you have any questions about the receiving process that aren’t covered here, please feel free to send them to me. I’d be happy to add them to the FAQs for everyone’s benefit.

entry.title: Re: Error in accessing cds view data using select query in Developer Extensibility in Cloud
entry.content: Hi prasanth, can you help to update the link? We are looking for an official SAP documentation regarding this.

entry.title: Re: SAP Commercial Project Management - Tables
entry.content:
Hi,

 

The main Table for Risk Management, Issue Management and Change Request is

Table = /IAM/D_I_ROOT
 
/IAM/D_I_ROOT - APPLICATION = RMA for Risk, ICM for Issue and Change
/IAM/D_I_ROOT - ISSUE_TYPE = document Type configured for Issue or Change and RMI for Risk
 /IAM/D_I_ROOT - ISSUE_CATEGORY = RMC for Risk, ICM for Issue and ICH for Change
 
/IAM/D_I_ROOT - OBJECT_GUID = /CPD/D_MP_HDR - DB_KEY
 
 
If Risk Management Assigned to Portfolio Item
/IAM/D_I_ROOT - OBJECT_GUID = /RPM/ITEM_D - GUID
 
Thanks
Abdul

entry.title: Re: Securing Advanced Event Mesh: Comprehensive Guide to OAuth Configuration in Broker Manager
entry.content:
Great blog, thx.

Thanks to this blog we were able to establish Entra ID authentication. Official documentation isn't clear and has some mistakes.

Eg. 

https://${ENDPOINT}/SEMP/v2/config/oauthProfiles/${PROFILE_NAME}

This doesn't work with POST because profile name is not needed for creation, (same for role mapping)

 

and better way is to use https://api.solace.cloud/api/v2/missionControl/eventBrokerServices/xxxxx/broker/SEMP/v2/config/oauthProfiles instead of ENDPOINT - ${CLOUD_HOST}/api/v2/missionControl/eventBrokerServices/${SERVICE_ID}/broker

Next thing OIDC well known URL is wrong 

  • SAP IAS: https://accounts.sap.com/.well-known/openid-configuration

correct one for IAS is as described in IAS guide https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/call-identity-authentication-discovery-endpoint 

A.

entry.title: Re: Learn how to work with SAPUI5 adaptation projects to extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi @elisabeth_riemann  - I am trying to complete the tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/sapui5-adaptation-change-variant.html 

In Step1, point 6 (shown below) I am unable to find the app F4596 Manage Credit Accounts in the list in the 'Select Application' field in the 'Adaptation Project - Configuration'. I am using the 'abap_trial' cloudready SAP system. Is there something I am missing. Could you please advise.

"From the dropdown list in the Select Application field, choose the SAP Fiori application that you wish to use as the basis for your application variant. Choose F4596 Manage Credit Accounts."

Thanks

Ranjit

entry.title: MIGO BADI POST DOCUMENT

how i should do it?

entry.title: Re: Historical PM Work Orders
entry.content:
Guys,

IS there a way to archive the work order which can transform to historical work order in the system so that we can access them using transaction IW38 and IW39?

entry.title: Re: SAP GUI for Java 8.10 on the Horizon
entry.content: @AbhishekSharma Raghug is correct. The include statement at the bottom of the landscape file will have the Windows location of the Global landscape file that must be deleted. I think that you made the change to the file that you imported i.e. not to the actual copy that SAP GUI made when importing. You should find this in /Users/<your_user>/Library/Preferences/SAP. Library is a hidden folder, use Cmd-Shift-dot to see it.

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content:
How to bring in SAP GRC Access Control User access requesting with Entra ID, so that we can send the request to SAP GRC AC for SoD checks.

for this scenario, we also may need to bring in BRM defined business roles to Entra ID, so that provisioning scenario can check SoDs in GRC.

entry.title: Re: Quizzes Options do not display - SAP Enable Now
entry.content:
Hi Anh,

Every quiz item (question) is based on a quiz template.  When you open a quiz in Producer and click on one of the items, you can define the template used for that question.  You will need to correct the formatting issue in the template.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Locate the template in Producer and open it.

After opening the quiz template in Producer, click on the Objects tab in the bottom, left corner.

 

 

 

 


In the list of objects, click on the object called quiz_element.

 

 

 



Open the Design section of properties and turn on the Font Color field and modify the color.  Save the change to the template and play your quiz again.

 

 

 

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Download Manager quits at the same second that i tried to open it

entry.title: Login to Integration suite, but the Menu didn't show up.
entry.content:
I install Integration suite, but the Menu didn't show up.

This concerning the Task 2 of "Learning Journey" on Integration suite

Please could you help me ?

In the exercie , i suppose to have Menu : see screenshot for exercise

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Sysdate On default Prompt Date on SAP Universe
entry.content:
@ferchi,  Modify your prompt code to use a DECODE or CASE statement.

= (add_months(DECODE(@Prompt('Date: ', 'D', , mono, free, persistent), '', system_date, @Prompt('Date: ', 'D', , mono, free, persistent)),-18)+1)

In other words, if the value entered for the prompt is empty, use system date, else the value entered.

entry.title: No authorization to access service group 'API_PURCHASEREQUISITION_2'
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have created an app in SAP BTP Business Application Studio, then i deployed the app. When I'm using it i got this error sending a GET request for the Purchase Requisition API.

But when I test it from Postman, everything works fine.

Can anyone help me, or have faced a similar issue?

Thanks,
Ines
